SurveyType.description:
Survey — Domain Entity (contexto surveys, "encuestas / votaciones").
A poll management opens to the property's residents (asambleas, decisiones de consorcio,
consultas). Residents cast one vote each while the survey is `open`; management `close`s
it to freeze the result (and may `reopen`). `single` accepts one option, `multiple`
accepts several. An `anonymous` survey tallies counts but never exposes who voted what.

CreateSurveyBody.description: HTTP body to create a survey.

Vote.description:
Vote — Domain Entity (contexto surveys).
One ballot per (survey, voter). Re-voting while the survey is open replaces the
previous selection. `unitId` records the voter's unit at cast time (context/reporting).

CastVoteBody.description: HTTP body to cast a vote.

SurveyOptionResult.description: Per-option tally plus the number of distinct voters.

BlacklistStatus.description:
BlacklistEntry — Domain Entity (contexto security).
A person a property has banned from entering. Enforced at the door: when a pass
is redeemed, a match (by name or email) denies access with reason `blacklisted`.

- `active`  → currently enforced.
- `lifted`  → no longer enforced (kept for audit instead of hard-deleting).

name.description: Human-entered name as typed by the operator.

nameNormalized.description: Lowercased, whitespace-collapsed name used for matching.

email.description: Optional normalized email used as a secondary match key.

reason.description: Why the person was blacklisted (free text).

AddBlacklistBody.description: HTTP body to blacklist a person.

AlertKind.description:
Alert — Domain Entity (contexto security).
An audit record of a security broadcast. Two kinds:

- `panic` → a resident pressed the panic button; staff/admins are notified.
- `mass`  → an admin broadcasts an emergency to every resident of the property.

Both deliver push regardless of quiet hours (they are emergencies).

GeoPoint.description: A coarse geolocation attached to a panic alert (optional).

triggeredBy.description: User id that triggered the alert.

unitId.description: Resident's unit for a panic alert, when known.

recipientsCount.description: How many users were notified when the alert fired.

PanicBody.description: HTTP body for the panic button.

MassAlertBody.description: HTTP body for a property-wide mass alert.

RingReason.description:
Ring — Domain Entity (contexto rings).
A doorbell event raised by an (unauthenticated) visitor towards a Unit.
State machine: ringing -> answered | rejected | timeout | cancelled.

answeredBy.description: Resident/admin userId that answered or rejected.

PhotoUpload.description:
Outbound port (DOMAIN layer) for persisting the visitor's photo.
Provider-agnostic: an in-memory adapter in dev/test, object storage
(S3, Cloudinary, GCS, etc.) in prod. The domain never imports an SDK.

RingInput.description: Visitor input to raise a ring (public; the photo is uploaded inline).

PropertyType.description:
Property — Domain Entity (contexto properties).
A directionable place visitors arrive to. Generalizes building/house/duplex/complex
so the same backend serves every kind of dwelling.

DirectoryVisibility.description:
Who appears in the public directory after scanning the QR.
- listed: visitors can pick a unit from the directory.
- code_only: directory stays hidden; the visitor must know the unit code.

code.description: Public short code embedded in the QR (unique).

directoryVisibility.description: Whether units are listed in the public directory.

createdBy.description: User id of the admin that created/owns the property.

DirectoryUnit.description:
Read model returned to the (unauthenticated) visitor after scanning the QR:
the property plus the list of active units to choose from.

UpdatePropertyInput.description: Partial patch accepted by the panel to edit a property.

PropertyQr.description: Shareable QR payload for a property: the public code and the deep-link URL.

UnitStatus.description:
Unit — Domain Entity (contexto properties).
A unit inside a Property (e.g. "4B", "Casa"). A house has 1 unit; a building, many.

label.description: What the visitor sees in the directory (e.g. "4B").

directoryName.description: Optional alias shown in the directory (e.g. "Familia Pérez").

BulkCreateUnitsInput.description: Bulk unit creation payload for the panel.

PropertyAdminRole.description:
PropertyAdmin — Domain Entity (contexto properties).
Quién puede gestionar una propiedad. A diferencia de Membership (residentes de una
unidad), esto es a nivel propiedad y con roles de gestión.

- owner: el creador. Control total, incluye gestionar admins. No se puede revocar.
- manager (administrador): gestiona unidades, residentes y ajustes.
- concierge (encargado): operativo (timbres/aperturas); NO gestiona ajustes ni admins.

ManageableAdminRole.description: Roles que se pueden asignar a un co-admin (owner queda reservado al creador).

MembershipRole.description:
Membership — Domain Entity (contexto properties).
Links a User to a Unit with a role. Used to know who to notify when a Unit is rung.

AccessPassKind.description:
AccessPass — Domain Entity (contexto passes).
A time-boxed, optionally recurring authorization for a named person to access a
property (and optionally a specific unit). It is the shared primitive behind both
resident-invited GUESTS (e.g. a cleaner every Monday) and management-invited STAFF.
The holder presents the `code` at the door; the pass is only redeemable while it is
within its validity period AND (when recurring) inside an active occurrence window.

Recurrence times are interpreted in UTC (a property-timezone refinement is future work).

RecurrenceRule.description: Weekly recurrence rule (MVP). Absent on a pass ⇒ continuously valid within its window.

interval.description: Every N weeks, anchored at the pass `validFrom`. Default 1 (every week).

unitId.description: Set for unit-scoped passes (a resident's guest); absent ⇒ property-wide (staff).

code.description: Redeemable, human-friendly code presented at the door. Unique.

validUntil.description: Overall expiry. Absent ⇒ open-ended (bounded only by recurrence/revocation).

CreateGuestPassBody.description: HTTP body for `POST /passes` (a guest pass scoped to a unit).

PassDenyReason.description: Why a redemption was denied (a structured decision rather than an exception).

ParcelStatus.description:
Parcel — Domain Entity (contexto parcels, "registro de paquetes").
A package received at the front desk for a unit. The concierge logs it; residents
are notified and pick it up (in-app or by showing a short pickup code at the desk).
State machine: pending -> picked_up | returned. Deliveries/pickups are mirrored in
the building activity log (access bitácora).

location.description: Where the parcel is stored, e.g. "Casillero Lobby · A1". Front desk when absent.

pickupCode.description: Short code the resident shows at the desk to pick the parcel up.

receivedBy.description: Concierge/admin userId that logged the parcel.

pickedUpBy.description: userId that took the parcel (resident in-app, or the verifier when redeemed by code).

remindAfter.description: When a still-pending parcel becomes eligible for an "uncollected" reminder.

reminded.description: Whether the uncollected reminder was already sent (avoids re-notifying).

RedeemParcelBody.description: HTTP body for `POST /parcels/redeem` (pickup by code at the desk).

CompartmentSize.description:
LockerCompartment — Domain Entity (contexto lockers).
A single door inside a locker bank. A parcel is deposited into a FREE compartment,
which becomes OCCUPIED and holds the parcel until the resident opens it by code.
`driverRef` is the opaque handle the access door driver uses to open this exact door.

propertyId.description: Denormalized from the parent locker for property-scoped queries and authz.

label.description: Door label within the bank, e.g. "A1". Unique per locker.

driverRef.description: Opaque reference the door driver uses to open this compartment.

parcelId.description: Parcel currently stored inside (present while OCCUPIED).

LockerStatus.description:
Locker — Domain Entity (contexto lockers, "casilleros inteligentes").
A locker bank/cabinet at a property, made up of compartments. The concierge deposits a
parcel into a free compartment (reusing the parcel pickup code); the resident opens that
compartment by code via the access door driver and the parcel is marked picked up.

LockerWithCompartments.description: A locker together with its compartments (read model for the panel).

CreateCompartmentInput.description: Input to provision a compartment (size defaults to 'medium').

LockerDepositResult.description: Result of a deposit: the created parcel and the compartment now holding it.

LockerPickupResult.description: Result of a pickup: the picked-up parcel and the freed compartment.

OpenCompartmentBody.description: HTTP body for `POST /lockers/open` (resident opens their compartment by code).

InvitationType.description:
Invitation — Domain Entity (contexto invitations).
An email-based, single-use invite to either become a resident of a Unit
(`unit_resident`) or a co-admin of a Property (`property_admin`).
Only the HASH of the token is stored; the raw token travels in the email link.

membershipRole.description: Role granted on accept for a resident invite (default `tenant`).

adminRole.description: Role granted on accept for a co-admin invite (default `manager`).

CreateResidentInvitationBody.description: HTTP body to invite a resident (route is not nested under the unit).

CreateAdminInvitationBody.description: HTTP body to invite a co-admin (route is not nested under the property).

UserStatus.description:
User — Domain Entity (contexto identity).
Pure business type. NO framework imports.

AcceptInvitationInput.description: Body for the public self-claim endpoint.

DevicePlatform.description:
Device — Domain Entity (contexto identity).
A user's device + its push token (FCM), used to deliver notifications.

ReadinessStatus.description:
Readiness probe payload. Unlike liveness (`/health/status`), readiness reflects whether
the app can actually serve traffic — i.e. its critical dependency (MongoDB) is connected.

ExpensePeriodStatus.description:
ExpensePeriod — Domain Entity (contexto expenses, "expensas").
A billing cycle for a property (typically a month). Management prepares charges while the
period is `draft`, `publish`es it so residents can see and pay, and `close`s it to freeze.

ChargeStatus.description:
ExpenseCharge — Domain Entity (contexto expenses).
What a single unit owes for a period. `amountDue` always ends in the unit's stable
`paymentCents` code (0..99) so an incoming bank transfer can be matched back to the unit
by its cents (see domain/services/reconciliation.ts).

baseAmount.description: Intended charge (whole pesos own the integer part).

paymentCents.description: Stable per-unit reconciliation code, 0..99 (the cents the resident must pay).

amountDue.description: What the resident must transfer: `floor(baseAmount) + paymentCents/100`.

AddChargeBody.description: Add/replace one unit's charge in a draft period (`amount` OR `items`).

GenerateChargesBody.description: Generate the same charge for every active unit without one yet.

UnitChargesSummary.description: A resident's account view for a property.

PaymentSource.description:
Payment — Domain Entity (contexto expenses).
A payment applied to a charge. `manual` = recorded by an admin; `bank` = matched from a
bank movement via the cents reconciliation.

registeredBy.description: Admin who recorded a manual payment.

PaymentResult.description: Result of applying a payment: the created payment plus the updated charge.

ReconcileResult.description: Outcome of trying to match a bank movement to a unit by its cents code.

cents.description: The cents extracted from the movement amount.

reason.description: Why it did not match, when `matched` is false.

ReconcileMovementBody.description: HTTP body to reconcile a single bank movement against the property's open charges.

QuietHours.description:
QuietHours — Domain Entity (contexto dnd, "no molestar").
Per-unit quiet-hours schedule. While active, doorbell (ring) PUSH notifications are
suppressed for the unit's residents; the ring is still recorded and emitted in realtime
(an open app still sees it). Times are interpreted in UTC (property-timezone is future work).

The window may wrap past midnight: `startTime` > `endTime` means e.g. 22:00 → 07:00.

byWeekday.description: Days the window applies: 0=Sunday … 6=Saturday (UTC). Absent/empty ⇒ every day.

ClaimCategory.description:
Claim — Domain Entity (contexto claims, "reclamos / tickets").
A support ticket a resident files to building management about an issue (maintenance,
noise, security...). Management triages it through a small workflow:

  open ──▶ in_progress ──▶ resolved ──▶ closed
    └──────────┴──▶ cancelled            (resolved/closed can be reopened → in_progress)

`closed` and `cancelled` are terminal except that a `resolved`/`closed` claim may be
reopened if the resident is not satisfied.

CreateClaimBody.description: HTTP body to file a claim.

AssignClaimBody.description: HTTP body to assign a claim to a property admin.

ResolveClaimBody.description: HTTP body to resolve a claim.

ClaimComment.description:
ClaimComment — Domain Entity (contexto claims).
A message on a claim's thread. `internal` notes are visible only to property admins
(staff handover), while public comments are part of the conversation with the resident.

AddClaimCommentBody.description: Application input / HTTP body to post a comment on a claim.

internal.description: Admin-only: when true the note is internal (residents never see it). Ignored for residents.

ConversationKind.description:
Conversation — Domain Entity (contexto chat).
A messaging thread inside a property. Two kinds:
- `unit_staff`: a unit's residents ↔ the building staff (concierge + management). One
  per unit (singleton); participants are resolved dynamically (current residents + admins).
- `direct`: a 1:1 thread between two affiliated users (e.g. an admin and a resident).

memberIds.description: The two user ids for `direct`; empty for `unit_staff` (resolved dynamically).

ConversationSummary.description: Conversation enriched with the caller's unread count (list view).

memberIds.description: The two user ids for `direct`; empty for `unit_staff` (resolved dynamically).

Message.description: Message — Domain Entity (contexto chat). A single text message in a conversation.

MessagePage.description: A page of messages, newest-first, with a cursor hint.

CallInitiatorKind.description: Who placed the call: an authenticated user, or an anonymous door visitor (no account).

CallStatus.description:
CallSession — Domain Entity (contexto calls).
A live audio/video call (WebRTC) towards a unit: an authenticated user calls a unit, its
active residents are notified, and the first to accept becomes the connected peer. The
server only relays signaling (SDP offer/answer + ICE candidates); media flows peer-to-peer.

State machine: ringing -> active -> ended  (or ringing -> ended).

ringId.description: Soft, opaque link to a `rings` doorbell event (not validated against that context).

initiatorKind.description: Whether the caller is an authenticated resident/admin or an anonymous door visitor.

initiatorId.description: The caller: an authenticated userId, or the synthetic id of a door visitor (`visitor:<ringId>`).

initiatorLabel.description: Display name of the caller when it is an anonymous visitor (carried from the ring).

answeredBy.description: Resident who accepted — the connected peer.

offer.description: Latest SDP offer (from the initiator), kept so a peer can fetch it on (re)connect.

StartCallBody.description: Caller starts a call towards a unit, providing its initial SDP offer.

VisitorCallToken.description: A short-lived bearer token authorizing a door visitor to place a live call to their unit.

IssueVisitorTokenBody.description: Request a visitor call token for a doorbell ring (public; the ring must still be ringing).

StartVisitorCallBody.description: A door visitor starts a call; the target unit comes from the visitor token, not the body.

RelayResult.description: Acknowledges a relayed signaling message (whether a peer was reached).

IceCandidateBody.description: A trickled ICE candidate, relayed verbatim to the other peer.

AnnouncementCategory.description:
Announcement — Domain Entity (contexto bulletin, "cartelera/avisos").
A one-way notice posted by property management and delivered to every resident of
the property (push + realtime, push suppressed for units in quiet hours). Pinned
notices sort first; an optional `expiresAt` drops the notice off the active board.

- `published` → visible on the active board.
- `archived`  → hidden (kept for history rather than hard-deleted).

expiresAt.description: When set, the notice disappears from the active board after this instant.

CreateAnnouncementBody.description: HTTP body to publish an announcement.

UpdateAnnouncementBody.description: HTTP body to edit an announcement.

BookingStatus.description:
Booking — Domain Entity (contexto amenities).
A reservation of an amenity for a time range, made by a resident on behalf of their
unit. A `cancelled` booking frees its slot (excluded from availability checks).

CreateBookingBody.description: HTTP body to reserve an amenity.

AmenityStatus.description:
Amenity — Domain Entity (contexto amenities, "reserva de amenities").
A bookable shared resource of a property (SUM, parrilla, gym, pileta...). Residents
reserve time ranges against it; `capacity` is how many confirmed bookings may overlap
(1 = exclusive). Optional `openTime`/`closeTime` (UTC 'HH:mm') bound the bookable hours
and `maxDurationMinutes` caps a single booking.

- `active`   → bookable and listed.
- `archived` → hidden / not bookable (kept for history rather than hard-deleted).

capacity.description: Simultaneous confirmed bookings allowed for an overlapping range (>= 1).

openTime.description: Operating-hours lower bound, 'HH:mm' UTC (set together with `closeTime`).

CreateAmenityBody.description: HTTP body to define an amenity.

UpdateAmenityBody.description: HTTP body to edit an amenity.

AccessPointKind.description:
AccessPoint — Domain Entity (contexto access).
A physical entry a property can open: main pedestrian door, vehicular gate, garage,
service entrance, amenity door, etc. Multi-point support is the base for proximity
opening and lockers. Each property has exactly one `isMain` point (auto-provisioned),
which is what gets opened when a request does not target a specific point.

`driverRef` is the opaque hardware address the IDoorDriver uses to target this point
(a relay channel, intercom id, lock URL key…). `location` enables proximity opening.

GeoLocation.description: Geographic anchor for an access point (used by proximity opening).

radiusMeters.description: Proximity radius in meters within which a nearby resident may open (default applied later).

isMain.description: The default entry for the property; opened when no specific point is targeted.

driverRef.description: Opaque hardware address the door driver uses to target this point.

createdBy.description: userId that created the point (the system/owner for auto-provisioned mains).

AccessEventType.description:
AccessEvent — Domain Entity (contexto access).
The building activity log (bitácora). Originally door openings; now also other
notable events (e.g. parcel delivery/pickup) so the whole building timeline lives
in one place. Discriminated by `type`. Append-only; never mutated.

type.description: What kind of activity this entry records (defaults to 'door').

openedBy.description: userId responsible for the event (opened the door / logged or handed over the parcel).

ringId.description: Ring this opening responded to, if any (audit linkage).

accessPointId.description: Access point that was opened, if any (audit linkage to the multi-point model).

parcelId.description: Parcel this entry relates to, if any (audit linkage).

OpenDoorInput.description: Input to open a door (the actor comes from the JWT).

accessPointId.description: Access point to open. When omitted, the property's main point is used.

ProximityCheckResult.description: Result of a proximity check: eligible carries a single-use grant to open with.

distanceMeters.description: Measured distance to the point (meters), when it could be computed.

grantId.description: Single-use token to pass to the open step; present only when eligible.

expiresAt.description: When the grant expires; present only when eligible.

info.title: skeleton-nodejs-tsoa-tsyringe
info.description: Node.js + TypeScript backend skeleton (DDD + TSOA + TSyringe + MongoDB)

get.description: Individual ballots — management only, non-anonymous surveys.
Readiness: can we serve traffic? Checks the MongoDB connection (503 if down).
A resident's own charges and balance for a property.

post.description: Match a single bank movement to a unit by its cents code and apply a `bank` payment.

get.description: The property's claim board (management only). Optional `status` filter.
The caller's own claims in a property.

post.description: Start or fetch a conversation (`unit_staff` needs `unitId`; `direct` needs `targetUserId`).

get.description: The caller's conversations in a property (newest activity first, with unread counts).
A newest-first page of messages; pass `before` (ISO date) to load older ones.

post.description: Mark the conversation read up to now (clears the caller's unread count).
Start a call towards a unit (provides the caller's SDP offer). Rings the residents.

Mint a short-lived call token for an anonymous door visitor (public). The caller passes the
`ringId` of the doorbell ring they raised; the returned token is scoped to that unit only.
A door visitor starts a call (authenticated by their visitor token). Rings the residents.

get.description: The call log for a unit (its residents or admins).

post.description: Resident answers with their SDP answer; the call goes active.
Trickle an ICE candidate to the other peer (relayed over realtime).
Step 1: verify the caller is near the access point; returns a single-use grant if eligible.
Step 2: consume a grant from `proximity/check` and open the access point.
